_protocol: Calibration topic — prospective. Tracks whether any post-2024 RTSC claim achieves dual independent replication within 12 months. Base rate near zero; field in high-skepticism posture post-LK-99.

meta.title: Next RTSC Claim Survives Replication
meta.question: Will any room-temperature superconductor claim published after 2024 be independently replicated by >=2 labs within 12 months of publication?
meta.resolution: Two independent labs (neither affiliated with the original authors) publish peer-reviewed or preprint results confirming both zero DC resistance and diamagnetic levitation (Meissner effect) at room temperature (>=15C) and ambient pressure (<= 1.1 atm) within 12 calendar months of the original claim's arXiv or journal deposit date.
meta.horizon: Rolling 12-month window from any new credible claim. Topic remains open until a claim fires or window closes without one.
meta.notes: Window is claim-triggered. If no credible claim is made, H4 resolves as TRUE at end of monitoring period (defined as 24 months from topic creation, i.e. April 2028). Ranga Dias retractions (2023-2024) and LK-99 debunking (Aug 2023) set the current epistemic baseline. Post-LK-99 replication speed record: ~2 weeks to first failure. Field discipline improved markedly.

tagConfig._docs: Science topic. EXPERIMENTAL = lab measurement published (moves H1/H2 up or H3/H4 down). RETRACTION = published withdrawal of claim. METHODOLOGY = structural critique of experimental design. DATA = numerical feed update. RHETORIC = press release, news quote, social media hype — does not move posteriors alone.

model._prior_rationale: H4 (no credible claim) is the base state as of April 2026. No credible RTSC claim has emerged post-LK-99 debunking. Historical base rate for genuine RTSC: effectively 0 in ~75 years of post-BCS condensed matter physics. H1 (full replication) requires both a new claim AND successful replication — a compound event. H3 (claim published, fails) is the canonical post-LK-99 trajectory if a claim does emerge. H2 (partial) reflects the LK-99 outcome itself — anomalous signal but not superconductivity.

H1.label: Claim published and replicated (>=2 independent labs, <=12 months)
H1.description: A credible RTSC claim is published AND two independent non-affiliated labs replicate both zero-resistance AND Meissner-effect levitation at room temperature and ambient pressure within 12 months. Would be Nobel-tier. Compound event: P(claim) * P(genuine|claim) * P(replicated|genuine). Each factor is small.

H2.label: Partial replication (1 lab only, or only some properties confirmed)
H2.description: A credible claim is published and one lab replicates some aspect (e.g. resistivity anomaly but no levitation, or levitation-like behavior without zero resistance). The LK-99 trajectory: striking partial signal, ultimately non-SC explanation. Higher than H1 because partial replication is easier to achieve and harder to immediately rule out.

H3.label: Claim published, fails replication (<2 independent confirmations in 12 months)
H3.description: A credible claim appears — passes an initial plausibility bar (specific materials, characterization data, Tc claim) — but replication attempts fail or find mundane explanations. The post-LK-99 default trajectory for any new RTSC claim given current field skepticism and rapid debunking infrastructure.

H4.label: No credible claim published within monitoring window
H4.description: No preprint or publication clears the credibility bar (defined as: specific synthesized material, at least two characterization measurements, authors with institutional affiliation) within the monitoring window. Current state as of April 2026. Field has gone ~32 months post-LK-99 without a credible successor claim. Dias retracted, lost Rochester position. Hype cycle in consolidation phase.

posteriorHistory.note: Initial priors. Anchored to post-LK-99 base rate (~32 months no credible claim), Dias retractions, historical RTSC failure rate. H4 dominant.
No new credible RTSC claims. Research roadmaps and screening tools are precursors, not breakthroughs. H4 (no credible claim) remains dominant.

redTeam.challenge: No significant shifts proposed; no challenge needed.

claimQuality._desc: If a new claim appears, assess its prior credibility before ingesting as H3-relevant evidence. A low-quality claim barely moves H3 vs H4.

materialsCharacterization.label: Structural characterization (XRD, TEM) published alongside claim

twoMeasurements.label: Both zero-resistance AND diamagnetic signal reported

independentDeposit.label: At least one co-author from different institution than PI

replicationVelocity._desc: If a claim fires, track how fast labs respond. Post-LK-99 baseline: first response ~3-5 days (simulation), first lab replication attempt ~10-14 days.

fieldSentiment._desc: Qualitative read on community prior. Affects how quickly debunking mobilizes.
fieldSentiment.rationale: Post-LK-99 field has established rapid-replication norms. arXiv moderation tightened for RTSC-adjacent claims. Major labs (MIT, Stanford, NIMS Japan, Max Planck) have standing protocols to quickly test new claims. Skepticism is high but not dismissive — community will respond fast.

_design_rules: Each indicator must be OBSERVABLE — a published paper, a press release with specific data, a retraction notice
Social media hype alone does NOT fire any indicator
Replication requires non-affiliated independent lab — same group, different campus still counts as affiliated
Partial credit (H2) requires published data, not just a claim of confirmation

tier1_critical.desc: Two independent, non-affiliated labs publish (arXiv or peer-reviewed journal) both zero-resistance AND diamagnetic levitation in the same claimed material, within 12 months of the original claim.
tier1_critical.posteriorEffect: H1 +40-50pp, H2 -10pp, H3 -20pp, H4 -20pp. Near-definitive H1 resolution.

tier1_critical.desc: A new RTSC claim clears the credibility bar: (a) institutional affiliation, (b) structural characterization published, (c) both zero-R and diamagnetic signal reported, (d) specific Tc stated. Triggers shift from H4 to active H1/H2/H3 contest.
tier1_critical.posteriorEffect: H4 -30pp (redistributed to H2/H3). Does not alone move H1 significantly.

tier1_critical.desc: Three or more independent labs publish failure-to-replicate results for the same claimed material, AND original authors do not produce rebuttal with new data within 60 days.
tier1_critical.posteriorEffect: H3 +30pp, H1 -15pp, H2 -10pp. Near-definitive H3 resolution if active claim exists.

tier2_strong.desc: One independent, non-affiliated lab publishes both zero-resistance AND diamagnetic signal in the claimed material.
tier2_strong.posteriorEffect: H2 +15pp, H1 +8pp, H3 -10pp. Significant but not sufficient for H1.

tier2_strong.desc: One or more labs confirm one property (resistivity anomaly OR diamagnetic behavior) but not both. E.g., LK-99 partial levitation without zero resistance.
tier2_strong.posteriorEffect: H2 +10pp, H3 -5pp. Mirrors LK-99 partial signal trajectory.

tier2_strong.desc: A peer-reviewed paper provides a specific mechanistic explanation (impurity phase, magnetic artifact, measurement error) that accounts for the original observations without invoking superconductivity.
tier2_strong.posteriorEffect: H3 +15pp, H2 -8pp, H1 -5pp. Strong debunk signal.

tier2_strong.desc: Original claim published in Nature, Science, Physical Review Letters, or Nature Materials (not just arXiv). Indicates peer review cleared basic hurdles.
tier2_strong.posteriorEffect: H3 +5pp, H2 +5pp (journal acceptance does NOT increase H1 on its own — post-Dias, journals have published later-retracted claims).

tier2_strong.desc: Original authors retract the claim before independent replication is achieved.

tier3_suggestive.desc: A new RTSC-adjacent arXiv preprint is posted claiming RT superconductivity but does NOT yet clear the full credibility bar (missing structural data, no Tc specified, or single-author with no affiliation).
tier3_suggestive.note: Low credibility claims are frequent noise. Do not move posteriors more than 2-3pp on a single weak preprint.

tier3_suggestive.desc: A named, credible lab (MIT, Stanford, NIMS, Max Planck, ETH, Cambridge) publicly announces it is attempting to replicate a specific new RTSC claim.
tier3_suggestive.posteriorEffect: H2 +3pp, H3 +2pp (indicates claim cleared internal plausibility check at major institution). Does not move H1.

tier3_suggestive.desc: DOE, NSF, DARPA, or equivalent issues a specific funding solicitation or grant for replication of a named post-2024 RTSC claim.
tier3_suggestive.posteriorEffect: H2 +3pp, H3 +2pp. Funding signal, not experimental confirmation.

tier3_suggestive.desc: DFT or other ab initio simulation published showing the claimed material's band structure is inconsistent with superconductivity at claimed Tc.
tier3_suggestive.posteriorEffect: H3 +3pp, H1 -2pp. Theory can lead experiment.

tier3_suggestive.desc: arXiv citation velocity for a specific new claim exceeds 50 citations in first 7 days. Indicates community engagement — consistent with both genuine interest (H2/H3) and hype (H4 slightly down).
tier3_suggestive.posteriorEffect: H4 -3pp, H3 +2pp. Community engagement signal, not experimental.

anti_indicators.desc: Six months pass since a credible claim with no independent replication of both properties from any lab.
anti_indicators.note: Temporal decay: H1 probability should compress further after 6-month silence. LK-99 debunked within 4 weeks.

anti_indicators.desc: Investigation opened or finding published by journal or institution regarding data integrity of the original claim's authors.
anti_indicators.note: Mirrors Dias (Rochester) trajectory. Misconduct finding is strong H1/H2 anti-indicator.

anti_indicators.desc: A claim clears the credibility bar (t1_claim_credibility_threshold fires). H4 can no longer resolve as the outcome.
anti_indicators.posteriorEffect: H4 set to 0 (topic restructures to H1/H2/H3 contest).

anti_indicators.desc: 24 months pass from topic creation (April 2028) with no credible RTSC claim published. H3 cannot resolve without an active claim.
anti_indicators.note: Temporal: if H4 persists to April 2028, topic resolves as H4. H3 requires a published claim.
anti_indicators.posteriorEffect: H3 set to 0, H4 +18pp (terminal H4 resolution signal).

actorModel.description: The outcome is determined by the collective behavior of condensed matter labs, preprint infrastructure, and the replication ecosystem — not a single decision-maker. Key actors operate on reputational and funding incentives.

claimant_labs.name: RTSC Claimant Labs
claimant_labs.role: Source of new claims. Incentivized by funding, prestige, and patent potential to publish rapidly.
claimant_labs.decisionStyle: Competitive, often under pressure to publish before rivals. Post-Dias, institutional review boards are more cautious but individual labs can still push premature claims.
biases: Pressure to claim RT conditions (maximizes media attention)
Sample selection: publish best sample, not representative sample
filters: Journal peer review (post-LK-99, Nature/Science tightened RTSC screening)
Institutional legal review if patent filing involved
arXiv moderation (now flags RTSC-adjacent claims for additional scrutiny)
overrides: National security / military funding can accelerate and shield claims from normal peer review
Pre-print culture bypasses journal gatekeeping entirely

replication_labs.name: Independent Replication Labs
replication_labs.role: Labs that attempt to reproduce claims. Speed and rigor determine H1 vs H3 outcome.
replication_labs.decisionStyle: Rational/institutional. Move fast when claim is high-profile. Post-LK-99, major labs maintain synthesis capability for common materials and can respond within 1-2 weeks for simple compositions.
biases: Publication bias toward positive results even in replication (partial signals may be published)
Anchoring on original claim's synthesis protocol
Reputational cost of premature positive replication (Dias case raised the bar)
filters: Funding and capacity constraints — only ~10-15 labs globally have full RTSC replication capability
Material availability (novel exotic materials may take months to synthesize independently)
overrides: High-profile claims trigger rapid mobilization regardless of cost
Counter-pressure from prior embarrassments (LK-99 hype) motivates skeptical fast-replication

arxiv_journals.name: arXiv / Journals
arxiv_journals.role: Publication infrastructure. Post-LK-99, arXiv added metadata flags for RTSC claims. Journals increased scrutiny.
arxiv_journals.decisionStyle: Rule-based, gatekeeping. Nature Physics added explicit RTSC checklist post-2023.
biases: High-impact claims still create publication incentive even with increased scrutiny
filters: Post-Dias: mandatory raw data and materials availability statements for RTSC claims
overrides: Authors can post directly to arXiv without review — journal gatekeeping only applies to peer-reviewed version

media_social.name: Science Media & Social Media
media_social.role: Amplification layer. Does NOT move posteriors but does influence how quickly replication labs mobilize.
media_social.decisionStyle: Sensation-driven. RT superconductivity is a reliably viral topic.
biases: LK-99 hype cycle now cited as cautionary tale — some dampening effect

funding_agencies.name: DOE / NSF / DARPA / ARPA-E
funding_agencies.role: Secondary influence. Rapid-replication funding programs can accelerate the H1/H3 determination.
funding_agencies.decisionStyle: Institutional, slow. DARPA can move fast if claim has defense relevance.
biases: Incentivized to fund any credible claim regardless of base rate — option value argument
filters: Political sensitivity post-Dias (DOE funded Dias lab)
overrides: DARPA discretionary programs can bypass normal review timelines

methodology: ACTIONS OVER RHETORIC — a press release about RTSC is noise; a published zero-resistance measurement is signal
TAG EVERYTHING — separate EXPERIMENTAL (lab data) from RHETORIC (media, investor statements)
PUBLICATION DATE MATTERS — use arXiv submission date as claim clock start, not media coverage date
AFFILIATION INDEPENDENCE — replication by collaborating institutions does not count; require genuine arms-length independence
COMPOUND EVENT DISCIPLINE — H1 requires both a credible claim AND successful replication; treat each as separate probability node
SOCIALIZATION DETECTION — watch for 'sources say a major lab is close' patterns; these are rhetorical, not EXPERIMENTAL

evidenceLog.text: LK-99 debunked: multiple independent labs (Beijing, Stuttgart, Maryland, others) find Cu2S phase transition explains resistivity anomaly; no zero-resistance; Meissner effect attributed to ferromagnetism. Consensus: not a superconductor. [HISTORICAL BASELINE]
evidenceLog.posteriorEffect: Establishes current baseline. H4 dominant entering 2026.

evidenceLog.text: Ranga Dias (U Rochester) third RTSC paper retracted by Nature. Pattern: three high-profile claims, all retracted. Dias removed from Rochester faculty. Chilling effect on aggressive RTSC claims in high-impact journals. [HISTORICAL BASELINE]
evidenceLog.source: Nature retraction notice; Rochester University statement
evidenceLog.posteriorEffect: Reinforces H4 prior. Reduces H3 probability (fewer credible-but-flawed claims expected near-term).

evidenceLog.text: Topic initialized. As of April 2026, no credible post-2024 RTSC claim identified in literature. ~32 months since LK-99 debunking. Field in high-skepticism, rapid-debunking posture. Initial priors set: H4=0.75, H3=0.18, H2=0.05, H1=0.02.

No room-temperature superconductor claim independently replicated as of April 2026. PNAS March 2026 roadmap calls for coordinated global push — notes no fundamental physics barrier but engineering challenge remains. Penn State zentropy theory (Oct 2025) enables computational screening of candidates. SLAC stabilized nickelate at room pressure (Feb 2025) but not room temperature. Highest validated Tc remains ~260K under extreme pressure.

GOVERNANCE WARNING on bayesian_update: confidence_inflation, stale_evidence, quorum_failure. Reason: No new credible RTSC claims. Research roadmaps and screening tools are precursors, not breakthroughs. H4 (no credible claim) remains dominant.

arxiv_rtsc_preprints_30d.label: arXiv RTSC-adjacent preprints (last 30 days)
arxiv_rtsc_preprints_30d.note: Credibility-screened count only. Baseline ~2/month post-LK-99 (noise floor of fringe claims). Spike above 5 within 7 days of any date warrants investigation.

citation_velocity_top_claim.label: Citation velocity — top active RTSC claim (citations/week)
citation_velocity_top_claim.note: Null when no active claim. LK-99 peak: ~300 citations/week in first month. Any new claim exceeding 50/week within first 7 days suggests genuine community engagement.

independent_replication_attempts.label: Independent replication attempts on top active claim
independent_replication_attempts.note: Count of distinct non-affiliated labs with announced or published replication attempt. Null when no active claim.

independent_replication_confirmations.label: Independent replication confirmations (both properties)
independent_replication_confirmations.note: H1 fires at >=2. Partial (one property only) feeds H2 count separately.

days_since_lk99_debunking.label: Days since LK-99 debunking consensus (Aug 12 2023)
days_since_lk99_debunking.note: Proxy for how long the field has operated without a credible successor claim. Longer elapsed time increases H4 weight.

watchpoints: arXiv cond-mat.supr-con section: any new RTSC claim with institutional affiliation and dual measurements
Nature, Science, PRL, Nature Materials: watch for RTSC submissions entering review or accepted
Retraction Watch: any new retractions flagged as RTSC-related
DOE/NSF/DARPA solicitations mentioning room-temperature superconductivity or ambient-pressure SC
University press releases: watch for institutional endorsement of a new RTSC claim (institutional backing was absent for many fringe claims)
Raman/XRD data accompanying any new claim: structural characterization is the minimum credibility bar post-LK-99
South Korea, China, India research groups: historically active in RTSC claims; monitor their condensed matter output
Hydrogen sulfide at high pressure (near-RT SC at very high pressure): if pressure drops toward ambient, watch closely
searchQueries: room temperature superconductor 2025 2026 new claim arxiv
cond-mat.supr-con arXiv new claim ambient pressure 2026
DOE NSF DARPA room temperature superconductor funding 2026
Meissner effect room temperature 2026 lab confirmation

predictionScoring._docs: Calibration infrastructure. Snapshots auto-created by engine on each posterior update. When topic resolves, record outcome in outcomes[] and run compute_calibration_report() for Brier analysis. This topic is part of the calibration set for the RTSC domain — used to assess model performance on science prediction tasks.
predictionScoring.calibrationNotes: H4 at 0.75 should be tested against base rate of 'no credible RTSC claim in any 12-month window since 2010.' Post-LK-99: ~100% H4 in every 12-month window. H1 at 0.02 reflects ~2% residual for genuine breakthrough — deliberately non-zero to avoid Dutch Book on a question where we have genuine uncertainty about unknown physics.

resolved.entry_a_text: Topic initialized. As of April 2026, no credible post-2024 RTSC claim identified in literature. ~32 months since LK-99 debunking. Field in high-skepticism, rapid-debunking posture. Initial priors set: H4=0.75, H3=0.18, H2=0.05, H1=0.02.
resolved.entry_b_text: No room-temperature superconductor claim independently replicated as of April 2026. PNAS March 2026 roadmap calls for coordinated global push — notes no fundamental physics barrier but engineering challenge remains. Penn State zentropy theory (Oct 2025) enables computational screening of candidates. SLAC stabilized nickelate at room pressure (Feb 2025) but not room temperature. Highest validated Tc remains ~260K under extreme pressure.
resolved.reason: Negation marker 'not' in new entry, absent from existing. Shared subjects: april, claim
